layman_headline: Fear grips the crypto market — patience beats chasing weak setups right now.
layman_explanation: The crypto market is in a 'Fear' state with Bitcoin dominance at 56.5%, meaning money is flowing away from smaller coins and toward Bitcoin. While several coins have dropped significantly, none of them are showing the clean reversal signals we need to justify risking capital. Most charts still look bearish with prices below their key moving averages. The best move right now is to wait for a clearer setup rather than catch a falling knife.
layman_action: Stay in cash for now. Set price alerts on Ethereum at $1,800 and watch for a confirmed bounce off that support level before considering an entry. Don't rush — fearful markets can stay fearful longer than expected.
expert_reasoning: I evaluated all 9 swing candidates and none present a compelling entry right now. BONK has a -2.0 chart bias with Three Black Crows — a classic sell signal, not a buy. PUMP surged 31.5% in 7 days and is likely extended. BinanceLife has a bull flag pattern (+0.80 bias) but poor risk/reward at 0.97x and RSI already at 58. LDO shows Three White Soldiers (+0.85 bias) but RSI is elevated at 59 and risk/reward is terrible at 0.38x with resistance only 7.4% away. ETH is the strongest fundamental candidate with RSI at 39 (approaching oversold, our most reliable signal at 80% win rate), but the chart bias is -0.70 with price below all MAs. High BTC dominance at 56.5% is a headwind for all alts. I'm waiting for ETH to test $1,800 support and show a confirmed bounce before committing capital.
risk_factors: BTC dominance at 56.5% continues to crush alt performance — could intensify further
Fear & Greed at 29 is improving but could easily reverse back toward extreme fear
CoinDesk reports a potential Bitcoin 'volmageddon' brewing, which could trigger a sharp market-wide selloff
watch_list: ETH price action at $1,800 support — a confirmed bounce with volume would trigger a BUY
Bitcoin volatility indicator flagged by CoinDesk — watch for sudden BTC moves that could cascade across alts
Fear & Greed Index trajectory — a move above 35 would confirm improving sentiment and support alt entries
market_context: The market is in a Fear state (29/100) with BTC dominance elevated at 56.5%, creating a hostile environment for altcoins. BTC itself is recovering slowly (+3.3% weekly) but alts are mixed, and most swing candidates show bearish chart structures below key moving averages.
context_banner: No high-conviction swing setups exist right now — disciplined patience in a fearful, BTC-dominant market protects capital for better entries ahead.

key_signals.description: ETH RSI at 39 is approaching oversold (our most reliable signal at 80% win rate), but hasn't hit the <30 trigger yet — worth monitoring closely.
Fear & Greed at 29 trending up from 25.9 weekly average suggests sentiment is improving but hasn't confirmed a reversal — premature to act aggressively.
Bitcoin 'volmageddon' warning suggests elevated volatility ahead — entering positions now carries outsized risk of sudden drawdowns.
Most candidates show bearish chart structures: BONK at -2.0, ETH/MON/KITE at -0.7. Only LDO (+0.85) and BinanceLife (+0.80) are bullish but both have poor risk/reward ratios.
ETH derivatives reset reported — could set up cleaner positioning for a bounce once spot price confirms support at $1,800.
